candidate_llm_application_summary: Single-model dalang scaffold: 3-slot persistent latent code (scene-frame, character-list, current-active-character); sub-token role switch by updating ONE slot; learned voice embeddings; single-model invocation throughout.

results.justification: Encyclopedia article on the source domain (wayang kulit shadow puppetry). Describes dalang, gamelan, puppets. No LLM-side mechanism. No overlap with LLM-side content words (dalang latent scaffold, scene-frame slot persistence, sub-token role switch, single-instance multi-persona).
Cultural article on wayang kulit and dalang's role; source domain only. No LLM-side mechanism, no overlap with scaffold/slot/sub-token/single-instance multi-persona content words.
Folk art museum page on the dhalang; source domain only. No LLM-side mechanism, no content-word overlap on LLM side.

keyword_overlap_terms: single-instance multi-persona (fixed-persona + multi-NPC from one model class)
scene-frame slot persistence (swappable memory module persisting per NPC)
results.justification: Fixed-Persona SLMs with Modular Memory: each persona has a fixed identity plus a swappable memory module; multi-NPC dialogue is deployed from one model class. This matches the candidate's swap-not-spawn single-instance multi-persona effect cluster and the persistent slot semantics. Functional equivalence is high (>=0.7); keyword overlap is also >=2 (multi-persona single instance; per-persona persistent memory slot). HIT under all three criteria.

keyword_overlap_terms: scene-frame slot persistence (personalized memory module + episodic+semantic memory persists across turns)
results.justification: PersonaAgent maintains a personalized memory module with episodic+semantic memory persisting across interactions; module conditions the model's behavior per persona. This is operationally near-identical to the candidate's scene-frame + character-list slot persistence with character voice embedding conditioning. Func>=0.7 and kw>=2 -> HIT.

keyword_overlap_terms: dalang latent scaffold (latent-space / latent memory family)
results.justification: Latent space survey covers many latent-memory methods (including MemGen) but is a broad survey, not a specific scaffold mechanism with 3-slot persistent code, sub-token role switching, or single-instance multi-persona. Only one weak content-word overlap (latent). Below all three thresholds.

keyword_overlap_terms: single-instance multi-persona (character generalization across personas in one model)
results.justification: OpenCharacter pursues character generalization via finetuning, producing one model that can simulate many characters. However it is text-prompt persona switching (cited by candidate as prior art) and does not implement a structured latent scaffold with sub-token slot updates. Func and sem below 0.7; only one matched content word. No hit.

keyword_overlap_terms: single-instance multi-persona (persona simulation in one model)
results.justification: Consistent persona simulation via multi-turn RL; aims for consistency rather than rapid sub-token switching among multiple personas with a structured scaffold. Below all three thresholds.

summary.verdict_rationale: Rank 4 (Fixed-Persona SLMs with Modular Memory, 2511.10277) and Rank 5 (PersonaAgent, 2506.06254) both exceed the functional threshold (>=0.7) and meet keyword overlap (>=2); total_hits=2 >= 1 -> FAIL.
